speaker: COMINIUS
text: Breathe you, my friends. Well fought! We are come off

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Like Romans, neither foolish in our stands

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Nor cowardly in retire. Believe me, sirs,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: We shall be charged again. Whiles we have struck,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: By interims and conveying gusts we have heard

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The charges of our friends. The Roman gods

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Lead their successes as we wish our own,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That both our powers, with smiling fronts encount’ring,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: May give you thankful sacrifice!

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Thy news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The citizens of Corioles have issued

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And given to Lartius and to Martius battle.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I saw our party to their trenches driven,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And then I came away.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Though thou speakest truth,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Methinks thou speak’st not well. How long is ’t since?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Above an hour, my lord.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: ’Tis not a mile; briefly we heard their drums.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: How couldst thou in a mile confound an hour

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And bring thy news so late?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Spies of the Volsces

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Held me in chase, that I was forced to wheel

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Three or four miles about; else had I, sir,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Half an hour since brought my report.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Who’s yonder,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That does appear as he were flayed? O gods,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He has the stamp of Martius, and I have

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Before-time seen him thus.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Come I too late?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: The shepherd knows not thunder from a tabor

speaker: COMINIUS
text: More than I know the sound of Martius’ tongue

speaker: COMINIUS
text: From every meaner man.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Come I too late?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Ay, if you come not in the blood of others,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: But mantled in your own.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: O, let me clip you

speaker: MARTIUS
text: In arms as sound as when I wooed, in heart

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As merry as when our nuptial day was done

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And tapers burnt to bedward!

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Flower of warriors, how is ’t with Titus Lartius?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As with a man busied about decrees,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Condemning some to death and some to exile;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Ransoming him or pitying, threat’ning th’ other;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Holding Corioles in the name of Rome

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Even like a fawning greyhound in the leash,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To let him slip at will.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where is that slave

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Which told me they had beat you to your trenches?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where is he? Call him hither.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Let him alone.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: He did inform the truth. But for our gentlemen,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: The common file—a plague! Tribunes for them!—

speaker: MARTIUS
text: The mouse ne’er shunned the cat as they did budge

speaker: MARTIUS
text: From rascals worse than they.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: But how prevailed you?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Will the time serve to tell? I do not think.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Where is the enemy? Are you lords o’ th’ field?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: If not, why cease you till you are so?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Martius, we have at disadvantage fought

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And did retire to win our purpose.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: How lies their battle? Know you on which side

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They have placed their men of trust?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: As I guess, Martius,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Their bands i’ th’ vaward are the Antiates,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Of their best trust; o’er them Aufidius,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Their very heart of hope.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I do beseech you,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: By all the battles wherein we have fought,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: By th’ blood we have shed together, by th’ vows we have made

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To endure friends, that you directly set me

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Against Aufidius and his Antiates,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And that you not delay the present, but,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Filling the air with swords advanced and darts,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: We prove this very hour.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Though I could wish

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You were conducted to a gentle bath

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And balms applied to you, yet dare I never

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Deny your asking. Take your choice of those

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That best can aid your action.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Those are they

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That most are willing. If any such be here—

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As it were sin to doubt—that love this painting

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Wherein you see me smeared; if any fear

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Lesser his person than an ill report;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: If any think brave death outweighs bad life,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And that his country’s dearer than himself;

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Let him alone, or so many so minded,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Wave thus to express his disposition

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And follow Martius.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: O, me alone! Make you a sword of me?

speaker: MARTIUS
text: If these shows be not outward, which of you

speaker: MARTIUS
text: But is four Volsces? None of you but is

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Able to bear against the great Aufidius

speaker: MARTIUS
text: A shield as hard as his. A certain number,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Though thanks to all, must I select from all.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: The rest shall bear the business in some other fight,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As cause will be obeyed. Please you to march,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And I shall quickly draw out my command,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Which men are best inclined.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: March on, my fellows.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Make good this ostentation, and you shall

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Divide in all with us.
